note: Prices and commission rates are directional estimates. Check each affiliate program's current terms before budgeting.

networks.name: Amazon Associates
networks.notes: 1-4% on most electronics; 1% on PCs and hardware.

networks.name: Newegg
networks.notes: ~1% on tech; higher on select categories.

networks.name: B&H Photo
networks.notes: 2-4% on cameras, computers, and pro AV.

networks.name: Best Buy
networks.notes: Low payout but high conversion on familiar brands.

networks.name: ShareASale / Awin
networks.notes: Marketplace with wide range; SaaS and DTC brands often 10-30%.

networks.name: Impact
networks.notes: Popular for SaaS, AI tools, and hosting; 15-50% common.

networks.name: PartnerStack / Rewardful
networks.notes: B2B SaaS referrals; recurring commissions possible.

networks.name: Direct brand program
networks.notes: Negotiated rate; often best terms for high-volume publishers.

faq.question: How accurate is this affiliate revenue estimate?
faq.answer: It is a directional model based on your traffic, click-through rate, conversion rate, average order value, commission rate, returns, and any network fees. Real revenue varies by niche, seasonality, audience intent, and merchant conversion rate.

faq.question: What is a good conversion rate for affiliate links?
faq.answer: It depends heavily on intent. Broad content blogs often see 0.5-1.5%, product reviews and comparison pages 1.5-4%, and email newsletters with strong recommendations 3-10%.

faq.question: Should I use commission percentage or CPA?
faq.answer: Use commission percentage when you earn a share of each sale. Use CPA (cost per acquisition) when the merchant pays a flat bounty per signup, trial, or lead. Some programs offer a hybrid.

faq.question: How do returns affect affiliate revenue?
faq.answer: Most programs claw back commission on refunded orders. The estimator subtracts an estimated return percentage from gross commission to show net revenue.

faq.question: What is RPM in affiliate marketing?
faq.answer: RPM is revenue per 1,000 visitors or sessions. It helps compare affiliate performance across pages with different traffic levels.
